name: matrix_debugging
description: Debugging and incident response: log triage, config repair, bug identification.

episodes.description: Identify root cause from crash logs — look past the stack trace to the upstream issue.

steps.name: analyze_logs
steps.input:
An application crashed. Here are the last 20 lines of /var/log/app.log:

2026-03-28 10:14:01 INFO  [worker-3] Processing batch #4471
2026-03-28 10:14:01 INFO  [worker-1] Heartbeat OK
2026-03-28 10:14:02 INFO  [worker-2] Processing batch #4472
2026-03-28 10:14:03 INFO  [pool-mgr] Active connections: 48/50
2026-03-28 10:14:04 INFO  [worker-3] Processing batch #4473
2026-03-28 10:14:05 WARN  [pool-mgr] Connection pool near capacity: 49/50
2026-03-28 10:14:06 INFO  [worker-1] Processing batch #4474
2026-03-28 10:14:07 INFO  [worker-2] Processing batch #4475
2026-03-28 10:14:08 WARN  [pool-mgr] Connection pool EXHAUSTED: 50/50 — new requests will block
2026-03-28 10:14:08 INFO  [worker-3] Attempting DB query for batch #4476
2026-03-28 10:14:09 INFO  [worker-1] Heartbeat OK
2026-03-28 10:14:10 WARN  [worker-3] Timeout waiting for DB connection (5000ms)
2026-03-28 10:14:10 INFO  [worker-2] Processing batch #4477
2026-03-28 10:14:11 INFO  [worker-1] Processing batch #4478
2026-03-28 10:14:12 ERROR [worker-3] java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke method on null connection
	at com.app.db.QueryExecutor.execute(QueryExecutor.java:142)
	at com.app.worker.BatchProcessor.processBatch(BatchProcessor.java:87)
	at com.app.worker.WorkerThread.run(WorkerThread.java:34)
2026-03-28 10:14:12 ERROR [main] Unhandled exception in worker-3, initiating shutdown
2026-03-28 10:14:12 INFO  [main] Shutting down gracefully...

Identify the ROOT CAUSE of the crash (not just the final exception). Reply as strict JSON with keys: root_cause (the actual underlying problem), exception (the Java exception thrown), fix (suggested remediation). No markdown, just JSON.

checks.rubric: The agent must identify that the root cause is connection pool exhaustion (50/50 connections used, new requests blocking), which caused worker-3's DB query to time out and receive a null connection, leading to the NullPointerException. The fix should address the pool issue (increase pool size, add connection timeout handling, implement backpressure, or fix connection leaks). Score 0 if the agent says the root cause IS the NullPointerException without mentioning connection pool exhaustion.

episodes.description: Find and describe three different types of errors in a YAML config file.

steps.name: fix_config
steps.input:
This YAML config file has multiple errors. The required schema: server needs host (string) and port (integer 1-65535), database needs host (string), port (integer), and name (string), logging needs level (one of DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR) and file (string).

Broken config:

server:
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  port: eighty

database:
    name: "myapp"
  port: 5432

logging:
  level: "INFO"
  file: "/var/log/app.log"

Find ALL errors (there are at least 3). Reply as strict JSON with keys:
- errors: array of objects with {error_type, description}
- fixed_config: the corrected YAML as a single string

No markdown, just JSON.

checks.rubric: The agent must find at least 3 errors: (1) port value 'eighty' is a string, not a valid integer — should be 80. (2) Inconsistent indentation under 'database' — 'name' has 4 spaces but 'port' has 2, making it invalid YAML. (3) database.host is missing entirely (required by schema). The fixed YAML should resolve all three. Score 0 if fewer than 3 distinct errors identified.

episodes.description: Identify a subtle rounding bug in Python code given failing test output.

steps.name: find_bug
steps.input:
Here is a Python function and its test results:

def safe_round_down(n):
    """Round a number down to the nearest integer (toward negative infinity)."""
    return int(n)

Test results:
  test_positive_integer ... PASSED     # safe_round_down(5) == 5
  test_positive_float ... PASSED       # safe_round_down(3.7) == 3
  test_zero ... PASSED                 # safe_round_down(0) == 0
  test_negative_float ... FAILED       # safe_round_down(-2.3) expected -3, got -2

Identify the bug and fix it. Reply as strict JSON with keys:
- bug_description: why int() is wrong here
- correct_implementation: the fixed function as a string
- explanation: why the fix works for negative numbers

No markdown, just JSON.

checks.rubric: The agent must identify that int() truncates toward zero (not toward negative infinity), so int(-2.3) = -2 instead of -3. The fix should use math.floor() which rounds toward negative infinity. The explanation must clearly distinguish truncation from floor for negative numbers. Score 0 if the agent doesn't mention the truncation vs floor distinction.
